channel.title: Arch Linux on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Arch Linux on Memo

item.title: Install Arch Linux with BTRFS (and Backup with Timeshift)
item.description: System # CPU: Intel UEFI Boot Commands # Keymap # Change keymap: loadkeys jp106 Time # Timedatectl: timedatectl set-ntp true Mirrorlist # Optimize mirror list: pacman -Syy pacman -S reflector reflector -c Japan --sort rate -a 6 --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist Disk Partitioning # Disk partitioning: gdisk /dev/vda /dev/vda1: EFI system (200M) /dev/vda2: Linux filesystem Format partitions: mkfs.

item.title: Install Arch Linux with BTRFS (and Backup with Snapper)
item.description: System # CPU: Intel UEFI Boot Multi-disk support with btrfs Devices: /dev/vda /dev/vdb /dev/vdc Commands # Keymap # Change keymap: loadkeys jp106 Time # Timedatectl: timedatectl set-ntp true Mirrorlist # Optimize mirror list: pacman -Syy pacman -S reflector # `python` might be required reflector -c Japan --sort rate -a 6 --save /etc/pacman.

item.title: Dual Booting on Multiple Disks (Windows10 and ArchLinux)
item.description: System # Preinstalled OS: Windows 10 (64 bit Home) CPU: Intel GPU: Nvidia SSD win linux Goal # Dual booting Windows 10 and Arch Linux Arch Linux with BTRFS Commands # Keymap # Set keymaps: loadkeys jp106 Mirrorlist # Optimize mirror list: pacman -Syyy pacman -S reflector reflector -c Japan --sort rate --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist Disk Partitioning # Find windows EFI partition: fdisk -l In my case: NAME MAJ:MIN RM SIZE RO TYPE MOUNTPOINT sda 8:0 0 931.

item.title: Dual Booting on Mono Disk (Windows10 and ArchLinux)
item.description: Preparation # Install Windows 10 Disk layout: Create partition for linux: Boot with iso Commands # loadkeys jp106 timedatectl set-ntp true pacman -Syy pacman -S reflector reflector -c Japan --sort rate --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist cfdisk /dev/sda mkfs.btrfs /dev/sda6 mkfs.fat -F32 /dev/sda5 mount /dev/sda6 /mnt btrfs su cr /mnt/@ btrfs su cr /mnt/@home btrfs su cr /mnt/@snapshots btrfs su cr /mnt/@var_log umount /mnt mount -o noatime,compress=lzo,space_cache=v2,subvol=@ /dev/sda6 /mnt mkdir -p /mnt/{boot,home,.

item.title: Desktop Environment
item.description: Comands # Graphic driver # Graphic card: # Intel sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel # nvidia sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Display server # xorg: sudo pacman -S xorg Display manager # lightdm: sudo pacman -S lightdm lightdm-gtk-greeter gdm: sudo pacman -S gdm Desktop environment # xfce: sudo pacman -S xfce4 xfce4-goodies gnome: sudo pacman -S gnome gnome-tweaks budgie: sudo pacman -S budgie-desktop gnome Activate display manager # lightdm: systemctl enable lightdm gdm: systemctl enable gdm

item.title: Configurtions for BSPWM
item.description: Commands # Graphic driver # Graphic card: sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms BSPWM # Display server, window manager and etc.: sudo pacman -S xorg xorg-xinit bspwm picom dmenu sxhkd nitrogen arandr Terminal emulator and brawser: sudo pacman -S alacritty firefox Create config files: mkdir .config/bspwm mkdir .config/sxhkd cp /usr/share/doc/bspwm/examples/bspwmrc .config/bspwm/ cp /usr/share/doc/bspwm/examples/sxhkdrc .config/sxhkd/ Change terminal emulator: vim /.

item.title: Installing Common Applications in Arch Linux
item.description: Package manager # Yay git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git cd yay makepkg -si Paru git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/paru cd paru makepkg -si Shell # zsh sudo pacman -S zsh zsh-completions chsh -s $(which zsh) # or chsh -s /bin/zsh Terminal # Alacritty sudo pacman -S alacritty Xfce4 terminal sudo pacman -S xfce4-terminal Gnome terminal yay -S gnome-terminal-transparency Terminal multiplexer # tmux sudo pacman -S tmux Editor # VSCode yay -S visual-studio-code-bin vim sudo pacman -S gvim emacs sudo pacman -S emacs-nox Backup # Timeshift yay -S timeshift Browser # Brave browser yay -S brave-bin Virtual Machine Monitor # KVM

item.title: Power Management
item.description: Install # Install tlp: sudo pacman -S tlp Check dependencies (Thinkpad only?): tlp-stat -b Install recommended package (e.g. acpi_call). Setting # When using btrfs as a file system: sudo vim /etc/default/tlp # /etc/default/tlp SATA_LINKPWR_ON_BAT=max_performance Start # Start tlp: sudo tlp start Systemctl: sudo systemctl enable tlp

item.title: Arch Build System
item.description: Install # Install asp: sudo pacman -S asp Usage (kernel build) # Create directory for build kernel: mkdir build cd build Download PKGBUILD via asp: asp update linux asp export linux Edit PKGBUILD: cd linux vim PKGBUILD 3c3 < pkgbase=linux --- > pkgbase=linux-custom 64d63 < make htmldocs 191c190 < pkgname=("$pkgbase" "$pkgbase-headers" "$pkgbase-docs") --- > pkgname=("$pkgbase" "$pkgbase-headers") Downloads source code: makepkg -so user@host linux % tree -C -L 2 .

item.title: Configurations for Spotify
item.description: HiDPI # Edit /usr/share/applications/spotify.desktop -Exec=spotify %U +Exec=spotify --force-device-scale-factor=1.5 %U Download error # ==> ERROR: Failure while downloading http://repository.spotify.com/pool/non-free/s/spotify-client/spotify-client_1.1.56.595.g2d2da0de_amd64.deb Solution: cd ~/.cache/paru/clone/spotify/ wget --continue http://repository.spotify.com/pool/non-free/s/spotify-client/spotify-client_1.1.56.595.g2d2da0de_amd64.deb mv spotify-client_1.1.56.595.g2d2da0de_amd64.deb spotify-1.1.56.595-x86_64.deb paru -Syu ==> Verifying source file signatures with gpg... spotify-1.1.72.439-3-Release ... FAILED (unknown public key 5E3C45D7B312C643) ==> ERROR: One or more PGP signatures could not be verified! error: failed to download sources for 'spotify-1:1.1.72.439-3': :: Downloading PKGBUILDs... PKGBUILDs up to date nothing new to review :: keys need to be imported:) F9A211976ED662F00E59361E5E3C45D7B312C643 wanted by: spotify-1:1.
